risk_if_not_implemented: Without Rogue Wireless Detection, security events may go undetected, allowing threats to persist unnoticed and increasing breach dwell time.

compensating_control_1.name: Wireless Networking
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to control authorized wireless usage and monitor for unauthorized wireless access.
compensating_control_1.justification: Wireless Networking (NET-15) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Rogue Wireless Detection (NET-15.5) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
compensating_control_2.description:
Automated mechanisms exist to enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for:
(1) Remote network access; 
(2) Third-party Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS); and/ or
(3) Non-console access to critical TAAS that store, transmit and/or process sensitive/regulated data.
compensating_control_2.justification: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) (IAC-06) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Rogue Wireless Detection (NET-15.5) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
